name: STK Proactive Command
description: Tag D0 is the PROACTIVE COMMAND BER-TLV tag per ETSI TS 102.223 §6.4. Any SMS-PP payload with D0 as first byte is a SIM Toolkit proactive command delivered OTA.

name: STK SEND SHORT MESSAGE command
description: Proactive command 0x13 = SEND SHORT MESSAGE (3GPP TS 31.111 §6.4.10). A SIM-initiated SMS send — can proxy attacker traffic without user interaction.

name: STK LAUNCH BROWSER command
description: Proactive command 0x15 = LAUNCH BROWSER (3GPP TS 31.111 §6.4.26). Used in SIM-based phishing (SimJacker / WIBAttack) to force device to open attacker-controlled URL.

name: STK SETUP CALL command
description: Proactive command 0x10 = SETUP CALL (3GPP TS 31.111 §6.4.13). Can silently initiate an outgoing call without user consent.

name: STK GET INPUT (credential phish)
description: Proactive command 0x23 = GET INPUT (ETSI TS 102.223 §6.6.3). Used in WIBAttack and SimJacker to prompt user for sensitive input (e.g., PIN/credentials).

name: STK SEND USSD command
description: Proactive command 0x11 = SEND USSD (3GPP TS 31.111 §6.4.11). SIM can initiate USSD dialout — used to exfiltrate data via USSD response channel.

name: STK SEND SS (call forwarding set)
description: STK SEND SS proactive command — can set unconditional call forwarding (**21*<attacker>##) silently.

name: SIM OTA Command (UDH IEI 0x70)
description: UDH Information Element Identifier 0x70 = SIM Data Download (3GPP TS 31.111 §7). Delivered via SMS-PP, the SIM processes this transparently without any user notification. Can install/update applets, read/write EF files.

name: SIM OTA Response (UDH IEI 0x71)
description: UDH IEI 0x71 = SIM Data Download response. Exfiltrates SIM applet execution results MO.

name: Pegasus stage-1 loader pattern A
mask: ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff
description: ASCII 'NSOGROUP' — found in Pegasus stage-1 payloads by Amnesty Tech (2021). String used in internal metadata of first-stage implant.

name: Pegasus network IOC domain pattern
mask: ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff
description: ASCII 'production' — appears in Pegasus C2 domain naming convention (e.g. production.X.Y). Citizen Lab (2018,2022) documented this pattern.

name: SS7 MAP Send Routing Info for SM
mask: ff 00 ff ff 00 ff 00 ff 00 ff ff ff
description: TCAP ASN.1 pattern for MAP SRI-SM operation code 0x45. SRI-SM queries the HLR for IMSI and MSC address before SMS delivery. Exploitation from rogue SCCP nodes enables subscriber tracking (GSMA TS08).

name: SS7 MAP Any-Time Interrogation
mask: ff 00 ff ff 00 ff 00 ff 00 ff ff ff
description: TCAP invoke for MAP ATI operation code 0x46. ATI retrieves real-time Cell-ID and LAC from HLR. Restricted to authorised nodes (3GPP TS 29.002). Known SS7 attack vector.

name: SS7 MAP Insert Subscriber Data
mask: ff 00 ff ff 00 ff 00 ff 00 ff ff ff
description: MAP ISD (opCode 0x07) pushes subscriber data updates to VLR. Malicious ISD can overwrite CallForwardingInfo to redirect calls to attacker-controlled numbers.

name: SS7 MAP Cancel Location
mask: ff 00 ff ff 00 ff 00 ff 00 ff ff ff
description: MAP cancelLocation (opCode 0x03) deregisters a subscriber from their VLR, causing service interruption. Denial-of-service attack vector.

name: SS7 over IP — M2PA link data
description: M2PA (RFC 4165) USER DATA message. M2PA carries MTP2 frames over SCTP. Presence indicates SS7 traffic routed over IP. Used for legal interconnects but also for remote SS7 access.

name: SCCP Unitdata message
description: SCCP message type 0x09 = UDT (Unitdata). SCCP connectionless transport used for MAP and TCAP messages. Rogue UDT messages from unverified OPC indicate SS7 flooding or protocol spoofing.

name: TCAP Invoke component
description: TCAP Begin/Invoke pattern. The 0xa1 tag is the Invoke component (ITU-T Q.773). Followed by operation code identifies which MAP operation was invoked.

name: WAP Push (port 2948)
description: WSP PUSH header prefix for WAP Push on UDP port 2948. Content-Type 0x02 or 0xAE indicates SI/SL. OMA-CP provisioning messages use this channel. Malicious WAP Push with OMA-CP can modify APN, proxy, or email settings silently.

name: OMA DM Bootstrap via WAP Push
description: OMA Device Management (DM) Bootstrap message delivered via WAP Push. The 0x01af content-type indicates application/vnd.syncml.dm+wbxml. Exploitation sends rogue device management trees to reconfigure device settings.

name: GTP-C Create Session Request
description: GTPv2-C (S11/S5/S8 interfaces) Create Session Request. Message type 0x20=32. Spoofed GTP Create Session from unauthorised nodes can hijack PDP contexts, redirect data traffic, or exhaust PGW resources.

name: GTP-C Delete Session (DoS vector)
description: GTPv2-C Delete Session Request (type 0x24). Forged Delete Session terminates active PDP contexts — subscriber data service disruption.

name: Diameter ULR (Update Location)
mask: ff ff ff 00 ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff
description: Diameter S6a/S6d Update Location Request (ULR, command code 316 = 0x13C). Used in LTE/5G HLR/HSS. Rogue ULR allows IMSI harvesting and subscriber location tracking via LTE core.

name: EICAR Anti-Malware Test File
mask: ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff ff
description: EICAR standard anti-malware test file signature. Used to verify scanner functionality.
